hints.suggested: Suggested from context or an external source; binding only after selection.
hints.confirmed: Confirmed and usable for next steps.
hints.conflicted: Sources differ; resolve before relying on it.
hints.fallback: Live lookup was not reliable; the offline index was used.

trust_grammar.esco_lookup_live_first_hint: ESCO uses the live API first; the offline index is available as fallback.
trust_grammar.esco_lookup_fallback_hint: Live lookup failed; the result comes from the offline index.
trust_grammar.esco_lookup_offline_hint: ESCO uses the offline index according to configuration.
trust_grammar.esco_lookup_missing_hint: No ESCO lookup has run in this session yet.

iceberg.aria_label: Iceberg model for classic and AI-supported recruiting briefs
iceberg.surface_label: above: visible in jobspec, job ad, and first briefing
iceberg.deep_label: below: essential for search, matching, interview, and offer acceptance

landing.headline: Turn role need into a recruiting brief.
landing.subheadline: For recruiting, HR, and hiring teams that need a shared decision basis before search, matching, interviews, and offers. Upload a jobspec or paste raw text; the app first shows what is reliable and which gaps slow the process down.
landing.value_line: Result: a recruiting brief cockpit with a role profile, priorities, open questions, ESCO anchor, and prepared recruiting outputs.
landing.subheadline_after_analysis: Next action: review detected facts, clean up uncertain points, and confirm the reference occupation.
landing.value_line_after_analysis: Already unlocked: role profile, gap prioritization, ESCO candidates, and next briefing questions.

company.headline: Position {company_name} as the employer for {role_title}
company.subheadline: Clarify company context, team structure, and positioning so recruiting and candidates understand why this role matters.
company.value_line: Helps explain why this role exists.

role_tasks.headline: Clarify what {role_title} is truly responsible for
role_tasks.subheadline: Prioritize tasks, outcomes, and success criteria so recruiting looks for the right impact, not just a list of activities.
role_tasks.value_line: Prevents recruiting from searching by title alone.

skills.headline: Separate must-haves from nice-to-haves
skills.subheadline: Build a testable skill list for matching, interview questions, salary forecasting, and the final job ad.
skills.value_line: Separates real requirements from wish lists.

benefits.headline: Describe the offer for {role_title} clearly and convincingly
benefits.subheadline: Capture salary, work model, benefits, and start conditions so candidates understand early why the role is worth considering.
benefits.value_line: Makes the offer comparable and negotiable.

interview.headline: Plan a fair interview process for {role_title}
interview.subheadline: Define interview stages, responsibilities, scorecards, and evidence so every decision stays transparent.

headline.default: The recruiting brief for {role_title} is {readiness_score}% ready
headline.ready: Ready for recruiting, interviews, and active sourcing

subheadline.default: Review remaining gaps, apply final adjustments, and generate the right recruiting outputs for recruiting, HR, and active sourcing.
subheadline.gap: Clarify or refresh the blocking items before exporting a job ad, interview guide, or search strings as final exports.
subheadline.ready: All important facts are checked. Generate the job ad, HR sheet, and search strings now.

summary.value_line: Creates directly usable material for HR, recruiting, and sourcing.
summary.empty_state: No analysis available for the summary yet.

public_pages.legal_review_notice: This page is binding only after subject-matter and legal review.

competencies.title: Our competencies

hero.lead: We professionalize the first step of every recruiting process: the recruiting brief. Unstructured input is turned into a clear, robust, reusable data set that improves search, selection, and internal alignment from the start.

competencies.meta: Focus: structured recruiting briefs, semantic quality, controlled AI use, security, and downstream processing

structured_intake.title: Structured recruiting brief
structured_intake.body: We do not start with the job ad, but with need clarification. This reduces misunderstandings, late correction loops, and overloaded wish lists early.

dynamic_flow.title: Dynamic question flow
dynamic_flow.body: The app does not use a rigid standard form. It derives exactly the questions that matter from the jobspec, role, context, and previous answers.

esco_semantics.title: ESCO-supported semantics
esco_semantics.body: Occupations and skills are not only captured linguistically, but anchored semantically. This improves comparability, clarity, and interoperability in international recruiting contexts.

controlled_ai.title: Controlled AI support
controlled_ai.body: AI is used where it creates structure and condenses content. Not as an end in itself, but inside a clear, traceable workflow.

salary_estimation.title: Salary estimation
salary_estimation.body: An indicative salary forecast makes visible how individual parameters influence compensation. This helps formulate roles more realistically and closer to the market.

exports.title: Downstream processing and exports
exports.body: The same data foundation can directly produce a recruiting brief, job ad, HR sheet, hiring manager sheet, search strings, and further recruiting outputs.

how.heading: ## How the app works
how.body:
The app starts with a jobspec, an upload, or free text. This starting point is first analyzed and converted into a robust structure.

Based on that, a role-specific question plan guides users step by step through further clarification.

The goal is not a longer process, but a better one: fewer unnecessary questions, less room for interpretation, and much higher reusability of the results.

intake.title: 1. Briefing instead of guesswork
intake.body:
At the beginning, existing role information is captured and structurally analyzed. The app deliberately separates source, interpretation, and confirmation.

This creates a robust starting point for the rest of the recruiting process.

**Value**
- fewer unclear starting points,
- fewer follow-up loops between the business team and recruiting,
- earlier quality assurance in the process.

dynamic.title: 2. Dynamic question flow instead of form overload
dynamic.body:
Based on the information already known, the app creates a **dynamic question flow**.

The most important topics appear first; additional depth opens only where information gaps exist or clarification is useful.

**In practical terms**
- minimal friction for business teams,
- higher input quality,
- better acceptance in daily work,
- clear separation between core information and details.

sharpening.title: 3. Sharpening instead of an impossible all-in-one profile
sharpening.body:
Tasks, requirements, skills, benefits, and salary logic are not only collected, but calibrated against each other.

This lowers the risk of formulating unrealistic role profiles that are barely fillable in the market.

**Result**
- more realistic target profiles,
- cleaner separation of must-have and nice-to-have criteria,
- a better foundation for later search and interviews.

esco.heading: ## ESCO as a semantic anchor
esco.body:
With ESCO, Cognitive Staffing integrates a Europe-wide, multilingual classification for skills, competences, qualifications, and occupations.

ESCO works like a shared semantic vocabulary that not only describes terms, but also makes their relationships machine-readable.
esco.callout_title: Why ESCO is valuable in this app
esco.callout_body: Instead of collecting only free-form role and skill labels, the app can semantically anchor occupations and skills. This increases consistency, comparability, and the quality of later derivations.
esco.column_a:
### What ESCO provides
- standardized occupations and skills,
- multilingual terms,
- API access for technical integration,
- relationships between occupations, skills, and knowledge areas,
- machine-readable concepts instead of plain keywords.
esco.column_b:
### Value for Cognitive Staffing
- cleaner occupation mapping,
- normalized skill suggestions,
- traceable origin of recommendations,
- better interoperability for search, matching, and reporting,
- more stable terminology across teams and locations.
esco.after: Because ESCO describes occupations and skills not merely as words, but as linked concepts, the classification is especially well suited for a structured recruiting brief.

model.heading: ## ChatGPT model use and AI architecture
model.body:
The app uses the OpenAI API **by task**. This means not every function necessarily runs on the same model.

Depending on the task - such as extraction, question planning, or output generation - routing can differ.

What matters is therefore not only the model itself, but the **controlled shape of the output**:
- structured results instead of plain prose,
- clear schemas instead of open-ended hallucination space,
- better downstream processing inside the wizard.
model.callout_body: Production configuration depends on the selected model and deployment. This keeps the architecture flexible without tying workflow quality to a single fixed model.

dynamic_flow.heading: ## Dynamic question flow
dynamic_flow.body:
The question flow adapts to:
- the submitted jobspec,
- information already identified,
- the selected level of detail,
- earlier answers in the workflow,
- and the confirmed semantic classification of the role.

This creates a process that works **adaptively** rather than rigidly.

Users see the essentials first - and more depth only where it actually adds value for the specific vacancy.

brief.title: Recruiting brief
brief.body: The consolidated decision foundation for recruiting, the business team, and management.

job_ad.title: Job ad generation
job_ad.body: The structured data collection becomes a consistent, audience-specific job ad.

interview.title: HR sheet and hiring manager sheet
interview.body: Preparation for HR and the business team with clear topics, checkpoints, and guiding questions.

boolean.title: Search strings
boolean.body: Derivations for LinkedIn, Xing, or Google so search strategies become more precise and reproducible.

exports.title: Exports
exports.body: Depending on the output, results can be reused as JSON, Markdown, DOCX, PDF, or mapping report.

security.heading: ## Security
security.body:
In an HR context, data sensitivity is not a side issue. That is why security is part of the product logic, not an afterthought.

Even in a cloud-based setup, structured processing, clear export paths, controlled model calls, and data minimization help handle sensitive content more deliberately.

local_llm.title: Local LLM as a security option
local_llm.body:
For especially sensitive HR topics, a **locally running LLM** or a strictly isolated on-prem/VPC setup can provide significant advantages:

- data remains in the organization's own infrastructure,
- access and storage locations can be controlled more tightly,
- third-country transfers and external dependencies can be reduced,
- security rules can be enforced company-specifically,
- acceptance for sensitive HR processes often increases significantly.

**Important context**
A local LLM is not automatically secure. It shifts responsibility from an external provider into the organization's own environment.

Implemented properly, however, it often offers more control, more transparency, and more governance for sensitive recruiting and HR topics.

cta.title: Would you like to see how an unclear hiring need becomes a reliable recruiting starting point?
cta.body: Try {brand} and experience how a structured recruiting brief improves recruiting from the start.

hero.lead: The company founder, Gerrit Fabisch, develops digital tools that help employers define processes more clearly and optimize them through the use of AI.

career.title: My background
career.body: I bring a profile that combines business understanding, interface competence, and current AI practice. After studying business administration, I worked for many years in sales- and recruiting-related roles - with responsibility for customer development, negotiations, project stability, KPI steering, and collaboration with different internal and external stakeholders.

offer.title: What I offer
offer.body: In my current work as a founder and AI recruitment consultant, I develop an AI-supported prototype for optimizing recruiting activities based on the OpenAI API, ESCO API, and a dedicated vector store. This is complemented by my data science training and LLM-specific courses on structured outputs and reasoning.

goal.title: What I am looking for
goal.body: I do not see AI as an end in itself, but as a lever for better decisions, more efficient processes, and concrete value for business teams and customers. I want to bring this mindset into an environment where AI is already anchored strategically and internationally.

why.heading: ## Why Cognitive Staffing
why.body:
Our strength lies in combining recruiting domain logic, semantic structuring, and modern AI support.

This creates solutions that do not merely look innovative, but noticeably reduce effort in daily work.

We think from the process perspective:
not from a single text, not from a single form, but from a better starting point for the entire recruiting journey.

cta.title: Our goal
cta.body: Not just faster hiring. Our goal is to bring employers and employees together in a way that is more fitting, more successful, and more sustainable over the long term.

intro.0: This page bundles the provider identification for Cognitive Staffing.
intro.1: Final reviewed company and register data is still missing for binding publication.

scope.heading: Scope
scope.body: This imprint applies to this website, the app interface, and all public information pages linked here.

required_info.heading: Mandatory information
required_info.body: The provider identification must contain the legally required information about the responsible entity, in particular name/company, address, contact channels, authorized representatives, and, where applicable, register or supervisory information.

responsibilities.heading: Responsibility for content
responsibilities.body: The content of this website is maintained with care. Timeliness and accuracy are reviewed when company data, contact channels, or app configuration change.

contact_paths.heading: Contact channels
contact_paths.body: Separate contact channels should be stated for general inquiries, privacy requests, and accessibility feedback where these are organizationally intended.

version_note.heading: Status and maintenance
version_note.body: The imprint should be updated promptly when company data, legal form, authorized representatives, hosting setup, or contact channels change.

items.company_address: full provider address with legal form and authorized representation
items.registry: register court, register number, VAT ID, or business ID where applicable

details.0: This page does not replace legal advice. Company data that has not been published is marked explicitly and must not be replaced with assumed values.

hero.lead: Protecting personal data matters to us. This page explains which data is processed when using our website and app, for which purposes this happens, and which rights affected persons have.

notice.title: Note
notice.body: This privacy policy describes the currently documented app configuration, contact channels, and data processing. Changes to service providers, retention periods, or internal processes must be reflected promptly.

privacy_contact.heading: ## 2. Privacy contact

processed_data.heading: ## 3. Which data we process
processed_data.body:
Depending on website and app use, the following data in particular may be processed:

- technical access data and log data,
- contact and communication data,
- content that users actively enter or upload,
- usage and settings data,
- consent and preference data where applicable,
- organization-related information in the context of app use.

purposes.heading: ## 4. Purposes of processing
purposes.body:
We process data in particular for the following purposes:

- providing and operating the website and app,
- handling inquiries,
- structurally preparing recruiting and vacancy information,
- generating downstream recruiting outputs inside the application,
- ensuring IT security, error analysis, and abuse prevention,
- meeting documentation and evidence obligations.

hr_content.heading: ## 5. Special notes on HR content
hr_content.body:
Our application can be used to process vacancy information, jobspecs, and comparable documents.

Please upload or submit only content whose processing is lawful, necessary, and internally approved.

Especially sensitive personal data should be processed only where there is a sound legal basis and an appropriate organizational framework.

legal_basis.heading: ## 6. Legal bases
legal_basis.body:
Depending on the specific case, processing is based in particular on:

- Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR,
- Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR,
- Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR,
- Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.

Where special categories of personal data are affected, the relevant sector-specific and data protection requirements also apply.

recipients.heading: ## 7. Recipients and service providers used

providers.ai: AI provider / LLM infrastructure: OpenAI API where AI functions are used
providers.email: Email / support workflow: configured contact email aliases
providers.consent: Consent / cookie management: Streamlit preferences and session state

recipients.body: Where external technical service providers or AI services are involved, this takes place only within the intended technical, organizational, and contractual safeguards.

retention.heading: ## 8. Storage and deletion
retention.body:
We store personal data only for as long as necessary for the respective purposes or as required by statutory retention obligations.

Where content is processed inside the application, processing should be limited to what is necessary and organizationally controlled. Exported documents and downstream recruiting outputs are also subject to the rules of the respective user organization.

cookies.heading: ## 9. Cookies and similar technologies
cookies.body:
We use cookies and comparable technologies only to the extent required.

Where technologies that are not strictly necessary are used, this is done only on the basis of valid consent or another applicable legal basis.

Further information is available in our cookie policy.

rights.heading: ## 10. Your rights
rights.body:
Affected persons have, within the statutory requirements, in particular the right to:

- access,
- rectification,
- deletion,
- restriction of processing,
- data portability,
- objection,
- withdraw consent with effect for the future,
- lodge a complaint with a competent supervisory authority.

security.heading: ## 11. Data security
security.body:
We take appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against loss, manipulation, unauthorized access, and other risks.

These include, in particular, measures for access control, limiting unnecessary data processing, secure configuration of the systems used, and traceable control of sensitive processes.

cta.title: Questions about privacy
cta.body: For privacy-related matters, you can reach us at **{privacy_email}**.

hero.lead: These terms of use govern the use of our website and the app functions provided. Please read the following information carefully.

offer.heading: ## 1. Subject matter of the offering
offer.body:
Cognitive Staffing provides digital functions for the structured capture, preparation, and further processing of recruiting and vacancy information.

The offering supports recruiting, HR, and alignment processes. It is not an autonomously deciding system.

no_advice.heading: ## 2. No substitute for legal or HR advice
no_advice.body:
The content and results generated inside the application are intended as professional support.

They do not constitute legal advice, a binding HR decision, or an automatic suitability assessment.

All results must be reviewed independently before further use.

permitted_use.heading: ## 3. Permitted use
permitted_use.body:
Use is permitted only in compliance with applicable law and only with content that you are authorized to process.

In particular, use is prohibited for:
- unlawful or discriminatory content,
- abusive or security-endangering purposes,
- processing impermissible or unauthorizedly submitted data,
- attempts to manipulate, overload, or circumvent technical safeguards.

user_responsibility.heading: ## 4. User responsibility
user_responsibility.body:
Users are responsible in particular for:

- entering only lawfully processable data,
- reviewing generated results professionally,
- complying with internal policies and legal requirements,
- ensuring appropriate safeguards in their own organizational environment.

availability.heading: ## 5. Availability
availability.body:
We strive to provide the offering as stably and continuously as possible.

However, there is no entitlement to permanent availability. Maintenance, further development, technical disruptions, or external dependencies may lead to limitations.

changes.heading: ## 6. Changes to the offering
changes.body: We reserve the right to adapt, further develop, restrict, or discontinue content, functions, and technical designs where there is an objective reason to do so.

ip.heading: ## 7. Intellectual property
ip.body: All content, trademarks, texts, designs, software components, and other protected elements of this website and app remain - unless stated otherwise - our property or the property of the respective rights holders.

liability.heading: ## 8. Liability
liability.body:
We are liable in accordance with statutory provisions.

For automatically or supportively generated content, we do not guarantee completeness, legal permissibility, commercial suitability, or absence of errors in individual cases. Users remain obliged to conduct their own review.

callout.title: Important context
callout.body: Cognitive Staffing supports structured decisions. Responsibility for professional, legal, and organizational approvals remains with the using persons or organizations.

final.heading: ## 9. Final provisions
final.body:
The law of the Federal Republic of Germany applies unless mandatory statutory provisions conflict with this.

If individual provisions of these terms of use are or become wholly or partly invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected.

cta.title: Contact for questions
cta.body: For questions about these terms of use, you can reach us at **{email}**.

hero.lead: We use cookies and comparable technologies only to the extent required. This page explains which categories exist and how you can manage your settings.

notice.title: Important note
notice.body: Current status: The app uses technical runtime and language settings. Marketing cookies, advertising trackers, and external analytics cookies are not part of this setup.

choices.heading: ## Your choices
choices.body:
You can adjust language and UI preferences directly in the interface. Browser-side storage can also be cleared through your browser settings.

Technically necessary storage is used where required for secure and functional provision of the website or app.

necessary.title: Technically necessary
necessary.body: Required for secure operation, navigation, session control, language persistence, and local draft recovery.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.body: Stores language, display preferences, and wizard settings so the interface remains consistent.

statistics.title: Statistics
statistics.body: Not active in the current setup. No external analytics cookies are set for traffic measurement.

external.title: External content / services
external.body: Not active as a cookie or tracking category. AI functions use server-side configured OpenAI API calls where triggered.

consent.heading: ## Managing settings
consent.body:
The app does not use a separate consent banner for optional marketing or analytics cookies because those categories are not active in the current setup.

Language and wizard preferences can be changed in the interface. Local browser data, such as stored language or draft-recovery metadata, can be cleared through browser settings.

transparency.heading: ## Transparency
transparency.body:
The current website uses Streamlit runtime mechanisms, session state, local storage for language and safe draft recovery, and a SameSite=Lax language cookie as fallback.

Stored draft metadata does not contain the full draft; the durable product contract remains the manual JSON draft.

current_setup.heading: ## Current technology use
current_setup.body:
- Language setting: local storage and SameSite=Lax cookie fallback,
- wizard and UI preferences: Streamlit session state,
- safe draft recovery: local-storage metadata while unsaved progress exists,
- marketing cookies: not active,
- external analytics cookies: not active.

cta.title: Questions about cookie settings
cta.body: For questions about technologies or preferences used, you can reach us at **{privacy_email}**.

hero.lead: We want to make our website and digital content as accessible and usable as possible. We orient ourselves toward recognized digital accessibility standards and continuously improve usability.

notice.title: Legal context
notice.body: This statement describes the current accessibility standard and known improvement areas. Whether BITV 2.0, BFSG, or further requirements apply directly depends on the specific operator and offering context.

status.heading: ## Compliance status
status.body:
This website is currently **partly accessible**.

We continuously work to further improve usability, clarity, and technical accessibility.

standard.heading: ## Our standard
standard.body:
We want content to be as understandable, clearly structured, and accessible in different usage situations as possible.

In particular, we orient ourselves toward:
- clear information architecture,
- good readability,
- contrast-aware design,
- consistent navigation,
- step-by-step improvement of interactive components.

implemented.heading: ## Measures already implemented
implemented.body:
- clear heading and section logic,
- compact, understandable texts wherever possible,
- consistent navigation patterns,
- ongoing review of contrast and display logic,
- continuous optimization of operability in dynamic interfaces.

barriers.heading: ## Remaining barriers
barriers.body:
Despite our efforts, limitations may still exist at present, in particular:
- for individual interactive components,
- for keyboard operation and focus guidance,
- for dynamically displayed or generated content,
- for documents or exported files,
- for more complex visual or data-driven displays.

feedback.heading: ## Feedback and contact

enforcement.heading: ## Enforcement or mediation information
enforcement.body: Where legally required or provided for in the specific use case, additional references to competent mediation or complaint bodies can be added.

cta.title: Report barriers
cta.body: We welcome specific feedback so that we can keep improving the accessibility of our content in a targeted way. Contact: **{accessibility_email}**

contact.title: Contact

hero.lead: Would you like to get to know Cognitive Staffing, request a demo, or discuss a specific use case? We look forward to your message.

contact.meta: For companies, HR, recruiting, IT, and product owners

decision_makers.title: Companies and decision makers
decision_makers.body: You want to professionalize the recruiting brief, reduce friction in recruiting, and enable better decisions earlier in the process.

hr.title: HR and recruiting
hr.body: You are interested in clearer handovers, better search profiles, stronger interview preparation, and reusable recruiting outputs.

it.title: IT and product owners
it.body: You want to learn more about architecture, security, integration capability, on-prem options, or local LLM scenarios.

privacy_notice.title: Privacy note
privacy_notice.body: Please do not send us especially sensitive personal data through the contact form or by email unless this is necessary and agreed.

form.heading: ## Request a demo or callback
form.name: Name
form.message_placeholder: Briefly describe your use case or request.
form.success: The request is prepared. Open your email app, review the message, and send it directly.

cta.title: Direct line
cta.body: For quick questions, you can reach us directly at **{email}**.
